title: Daily Market Digest: June 27, 2026
subtitle: Market closes mixed amid tech sell-off and sector rotation

items.headline: Tech Sector Retreats, Broader Market Shows Resilience
items.summary: U.S. equities closed a mixed week as major technology and AI-related stocks faced significant selling pressure, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average posted a weekly gain. Investors appear to be rotating into more defensive sectors and smaller cap stocks, indicating a shift in market leadership. Despite a tech sell-off, the broader market showed signs of resilience, with the equal-weighted S&P 500 outperforming its market-cap weighted counterpart. Concerns over AI valuations and future Fed policy continue to shape sentiment.

items.statContext: Down 0.05% on June 26, weekly loss of 2%
Down 0.24% on June 26, weekly loss of 4.6%
Down 0.09% on June 26, weekly gain of 0.6%

items.headline: Micron Technology
items.summary: Shares fell almost 7% on June 26 as part of a broader sell-off in memory and chipmakers, despite earlier reports of explosive Q3 earnings that surpassed analyst estimates.

items.headline: Nvidia
items.summary: Continued its weekly downturn, falling 1.6% on June 26, contributing to an 8.6% weekly decline amidst concerns over AI valuations.

items.headline: Apogee Enterprises
items.summary: Hit a new 52-week high after reporting better-than-expected quarterly earnings, exceeding EPS and revenue forecasts for its fiscal Q1 2027.

items.headline: Eli Lilly
items.summary: Gained 7% on June 26, benefiting from a rotation into the healthcare sector amidst broader market shifts.

items.headline: Oracle
items.summary: Plummeted 19% this week, marking its steepest weekly drop since 2001, reportedly due to costs associated with its AI infrastructure commitments.

items.headline: Caterpillar
items.summary: Surged 6% during the past week, contributing to the Dow's overall gain and reflecting strength in industrial sectors.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Releases Annual Stress Test Results
items.summary: The Federal Reserve released its annual stress test results, indicating that large banks are resilient enough to withstand a severe recession, with capital declining only 1.6 percentage points in aggregate despite over $708 billion in loan losses under the hypothetical scenario. All 32 banks tested remained above minimum capital requirements.

items.headline: FOMC Dot Plot Signals Hawkish Shift, Rate Hike Expected
items.summary: Despite a unanimous decision on June 17, 2026, to hold the federal funds rate at 3.50%-3.75%, the FOMC's 'dot plot' now suggests a rate hike before year-end, according to Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari. This marks a notable shift from earlier expectations of rate cuts, driven by persistent inflation and the robust AI boom.

items.headline: Investors Rotate into Defensive and Small-Cap Sectors
items.summary: A clear rotation out of high-flying technology and AI stocks was observed, with Healthcare, Real Estate, and Utilities emerging as top-performing S&P 500 sectors. Small-cap stocks also showed impressive strength, with the S&P 600 Small Cap Index closing at a record high, as investors seek more stable areas of the market.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Released for May/June
items.summary: The U.S. goods trade deficit widened by 27.4% in May, reaching $105.8 billion. In contrast, the University of Michigan's consumer sentiment index for June was revised upward to 49.5, and both short-term and long-term inflation expectations saw declines.

items.headline: AI Bubble Concerns Intensify Amid Tech Weakness
items.summary: Amid a significant sell-off in semiconductor and AI-related stocks, concerns about a potential 'AI bubble burst' are intensifying. Oracle's 19% weekly decline, linked to AI infrastructure costs, highlights the financial pressures in the sector, leading investors to question valuations.
